# Zurich API Reference

- [API implementation and reference](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/api-implementation-reference.md) -- Get started using ServiceNow APIs with guides, resources, and reference documentation.
  - [API implementation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/api-implementation.md) -- You can use JavaScript APIs to extend application server and client functionality. Use web services to connect ServiceNow applications to other software applications.
    - [Scripting](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_Script.md) -- Use scripts to extend your instance beyond standard configurations. With scripts, you may automate processes, add functionality, integrate your instance with an outside application and more.
      - [Available script types](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/r_17Scripts.md) -- Scripts can be used in many places. The most important detail is whether the script runs on the client or the server.
      - [Glide class overview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/r_GlideClassOverview.md) -- The ServiceNow Glide classes expose JavaScript APIs that enable you to conveniently work with tables using scripts.
        - [Glide stack](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/r_GlideStack.md) -- Glide is an extensible Web 2.0 development platform written in Java that facilitates rapid development of forms-based workflow applications (work orders, trouble ticketing, and project management, for example).
      - [Execution order](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/r_ExecutionOrderScriptsAndEngines.md) -- Scripts, assignment rules, business rules, workflows, escalations, and engines all take effect in relation to a database operation, such as insert or update. In many cases, the order of these events is important.
      - [Script evaluation by data type](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/r_ScriptingOfFieldTypes.md) -- Script fields evaluate data based on the field type of the input.
      - [Scripting messages](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/r_ScriptingAlertInfoAndErrorMsgs.md) -- You can send messages to customers as alerts, informational messages, or error messages.
      - [Now Assist for Code](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/now-assist-code-landing.md) -- ServiceNow Now Assist for Code is a generative AI-powered tool that assists you by providing code suggestions. It accelerates the coding tasks and boosts productivity when scripting on the ServiceNow AI Platform.
        - [Explore Now Assist for Code](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/exploring-now-assist-code.md) -- ServiceNow Now Assist for Code is a generative AI-powered tool designed to help developers create, edit, and summarize code.
          - [General guidelines](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/general-guidelines-code-generation.md) -- Use these general guidelines for code generation to get better code suggestions and create useful and accurate scripts.
        - [Configure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/configuring-now-assist-code.md) -- Install and configure Now Assist for Code on an instance.
          - [Install](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/install-now-assist-code-app.md) -- Install the ServiceNow Now Assist for Creator application from the ServiceNow Store to get Now Assist for code generation.
          - [Configure AI model providers](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/configure-model-providers-for-now-assist-skills.md) -- Select a large language model (LLM) as the AI service provider for Now Assist for Code skills.
            - [Set model providers for instance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/configure-model-providers-for-now-assist-skills.md) -- Select a large language model (LLM) as the AI service provider for Now Assist for Code skills.
            - [Select AI model provider in the script editor](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/configure-model-providers-for-now-assist-skills.md) -- Select a large language model (LLM) as the AI service provider for Now Assist for Code skills.
          - [Enable or disable Now Assist skills](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/enable-or-disable-now-assist-for-code-skills.md) -- Learn to enable and disable Now Assist for Code skills.
        - [Use Now Assist for Code](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/using-now-assist-code.md) -- Learn to use ServiceNowNow Assist for Code to generate, edit, explain and summarize code.
          - [Generate code](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/generate-scripts-from-text.md) -- Generate code from text with AI-powered Now Assist for Code.
          - [Code autocomplete](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/generate-code-with-autocomplete.md) -- The autocomplete feature of Now Assist for Code provides you with contextually relevant code suggestions.
          - [Code edit](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/edit-code-now-assist.md) -- Quickly enhance your scripts by instructing Now Assist on how to improve the code.
          - [Add comments](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/add-comments-to-code-with-quick-actions.md) -- Add comments in the Now Assist for Code enabled script editor using quick actions.
          - [Explain and summarize code](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/explain-and-summarize-code-with-quick-actions.md) -- Get a summary of the code or a comprehensive explanation of its functionality.
          - [Tracking AI-generated code](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/tracking-ai-generated-code.md) -- Control when to track and indicate that code is AI-generated.
            - [Track AI-generate code](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/tracking-ai-generated-code.md) -- Control when to track and indicate that code is AI-generated.
            - [Turn off tracking AI-generated code](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/tracking-ai-generated-code.md) -- Control when to track and indicate that code is AI-generated.
        - [Now Assist for Code reference](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/now-assist-code-reference.md) -- Reference topics provide additional information about configuration properties, roles, and more.
          - [Now Assist for Code properties](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/now-assist-code-properties.md) -- You can adjust how code generation functions on an instance using several advanced properties.
          - [Now Assist for Code roles](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/now-assist-code-roles.md) -- Understand the roles needed to use Now Assist for Code. These roles are created when Now Assist for Creator is installed.
          - [Keyboard shortcuts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/keyboard-shortcuts.md) -- Learn about the keyboard shortcuts that you can use in the script editor on the ServiceNow AI Platform.
      - [JavaScript syntax editor](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_SyntaxEditor.md) -- The JavaScript syntax editor provides support for editing JavaScript scripts.
        - [Using the JavaScript syntax editor](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/r_EdtJvaScptWSyntxEdtr.md) -- The syntax editor provides editing functions to support editing JavaScript scripts.
          - [Keyboard shortcuts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/r_SyntxEdtrKybrdSrtctsActn.md) -- The syntax editor offers keyboard shortcuts and actions to assist in writing code.
          - [Script macros](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/r_SyntaxEditorMacros.md) -- Script macros provide shortcuts for typing commonly used code. To insert macro text into a script field, enter the macro keyword followed by the Tab.
          - [Context menu options](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/context-menu-syntax-editor.md) -- View the context menu for script includes, Glide APIs, and tables in the JavaScript syntax editor.
          - [Syntax errors and warnings](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ScriptSyntaxErrorChecking.md) -- All script fields provide controls for checking the syntax for errors and for locating the error easily when one occurs.
        - [Create a script macro](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/t_ManageScriptMacros.md) -- Administrators can define new script macros or modify existing script macros.
      - [HTML syntax editor](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/html-syntax-editor.md) -- The HTML syntax editor provides support for editing HTML and Jelly scripts and defines what's rendered when the page is displayed. The HTML syntax editor can contain either static XHTML or dynamically generated content defined as Jelly, and can call script includes and UI Macros.
      - [Code editor](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/monaco-editor.md) -- The code editor provides support to use programming language services in a text editor and is used in scripts.
      - [Server-side scripting](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ServerScripting.md) -- Server scripts run on the server or database. They can change the appearance or behavior of ServiceNow or run as business rules when records and tables are accessed or modified.
        - [Glide Server APIs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/p_GlideServerAPIs.md) -- ServiceNow provides APIs for the Glide Server.

        - [Classic Business rules](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/business-rules-classic/c_BusinessRules.md) -- A business rule is a server-side script that runs when a record is displayed, inserted, updated, or deleted, or when a table is queried.

        - [Script includes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ScriptIncludes.md) -- Script includes are used to store JavaScript that runs on the server.

        - [Processors](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_Processors.md) -- Processors provide a customizable URL endpoint that can execute arbitrary server-side JavaScript code and produce output such as TEXT or JSON. Creating custom processors is deprecated.
          - [API components](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/r_ProcessorAPIComponents.md) -- Processors have access to dedicated API classes, objects, and methods.
          - [Security](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/t_SecureAProcessor.md) -- You can protect your processor against unauthorized use by using role restrictions, and protect it by requiring a CSRF token.
            - [Require a CSRF token](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/t_SecureAProcessor.md) -- You can protect your processor against unauthorized use by using role restrictions, and protect it by requiring a CSRF token.
          - [Create: simple](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/t_CreateSimpleProcessor.md) -- Create a simple processor to execute a script from a URL query. This feature is deprecated.
          - [Create: multi-table](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/t_CreateMultiTableProcessor.md) -- Create a multi-table processor that reports the number of rows in any table on your instance. This feature is deprecated.
          - [Create: custom](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/t_CreatingACustomProcessor.md) -- Create a custom processor to execute a script from a URL query. This feature is deprecated.
        - [Scripts - Background module](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ScriptsBackground.md) -- Administrators can use the Scripts - Background module to run arbitrary JavaScript code from the server.
        - [Sandbox environment](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/script-sandbox-environment.md) -- The script sandbox environment is a restricted execution context in which untrusted, client-generated scripts run on the server using one of two evaluators: the guarded script evaluator or the script sandbox evaluator.
          - [Guarded script evaluator](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/guarded-script.md) -- The guarded script evaluator enhances instance security by supporting only a restricted scripting language and detecting or rejecting untrusted scripts that use unsupported JavaScript features.
            - [Reviewing scripts incompatible with guarded script](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/incompatible-guarded-scripts.md) -- Guarded script records scripts that use unsupported JavaScript features in the Incompatible Guarded Scripts list when transactions calling those scripts are sent to the server.
              - [Update incompatible guarded scripts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/incompatible-guarded-scripts.md) -- Guarded script records scripts that use unsupported JavaScript features in the Incompatible Guarded Scripts list when transactions calling those scripts are sent to the server.
            - [Guarded script API support](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/guarded-script-allowed-apis.md) -- Review the JavaScript APIs that guarded script supports to help you analyze scripts in the Incompatible Guarded Scripts list and either rewrite them or create an exemption for them.
          - [Script sandbox evaluator](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/script-sandbox.md) -- The script sandbox evaluator helps prevent executing untrusted scripts on an instance by limiting the APIs available to scripts.
        - [Installation settings](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_InstallationSettings.md) -- Installation settings are global business rules with calculated names. Installation settings are calculated just before a record is displayed and facilitate dynamic determination of access and roles. Installation Settings permit the programmatic determination of a setting.
        - [Calculating a due date](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_DrtnClDueDate.md) -- Using the DurationCalculator script include, you can calculate a due date, using either a simple duration or a relative duration base on schedules.
          - [Computing a simple duration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_DrtnClDueDate.md) -- Using the DurationCalculator script include, you can calculate a due date, using either a simple duration or a relative duration base on schedules.
          - [Using relative duration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_DrtnClDueDate.md) -- Using the DurationCalculator script include, you can calculate a due date, using either a simple duration or a relative duration base on schedules.
        - [Querying tables](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_UsingGlideRecordToQueryTables.md) -- Using methods in the GlideRecord API, you can return all records from a table, return records from a table that satisfy specific conditions, or return records that include a string from a single table or from multiple tables in a text index group.
          - [Available JavaScript operators](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_UsingGlideRecordToQueryTables.md) -- Using methods in the GlideRecord API, you can return all records from a table, return records from a table that satisfy specific conditions, or return records that include a string from a single table or from multiple tables in a text index group.
          - [Examples](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_UsingGlideRecordToQueryTables.md) -- Using methods in the GlideRecord API, you can return all records from a table, return records from a table that satisfy specific conditions, or return records that include a string from a single table or from multiple tables in a text index group.
        - [Running order guides automatically](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_RunningOrderGuidesAutomatically.md) -- Service catalog order guides allow customers to make a single service catalog request that can generate several ordered items. Administrators can configure order guides to run automatically, from a workflow or a script to generate a set of ordered items without manually submitting a service catalog request. Administrators can also review and reprocess the order guide failures.
          - [Running from scripts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_RunningOrderGuidesAutomatically.md) -- Service catalog order guides allow customers to make a single service catalog request that can generate several ordered items. Administrators can configure order guides to run automatically, from a workflow or a script to generate a set of ordered items without manually submitting a service catalog request. Administrators can also review and reprocess the order guide failures.
          - [Running from workflows](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_RunningOrderGuidesAutomatically.md) -- Service catalog order guides allow customers to make a single service catalog request that can generate several ordered items. Administrators can configure order guides to run automatically, from a workflow or a script to generate a set of ordered items without manually submitting a service catalog request. Administrators can also review and reprocess the order guide failures.
          - [View failures](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_RunningOrderGuidesAutomatically.md) -- Service catalog order guides allow customers to make a single service catalog request that can generate several ordered items. Administrators can configure order guides to run automatically, from a workflow or a script to generate a set of ordered items without manually submitting a service catalog request. Administrators can also review and reprocess the order guide failures.
        - [Assigning execution plans](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ScptblAsmtExctnPlns.md) -- Each catalog item has an associated execution plan, used whenever an item of that type is ordered; if no plan is specified, the default plan is used. This default is effective for most organizations, but your execution plan may need to vary based on additional criteria.
          - [Limitations](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ScptblAsmtExctnPlns.md) -- Each catalog item has an associated execution plan, used whenever an item of that type is ordered; if no plan is specified, the default plan is used. This default is effective for most organizations, but your execution plan may need to vary based on additional criteria.

This default is effective for most organizations, but your execution plan may need to vary based on additional criteria.
          - [Add a catalog item script](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ScptblAsmtExctnPlns.md) -- Each catalog item has an associated execution plan, used whenever an item of that type is ordered; if no plan is specified, the default plan is used. This default is effective for most organizations, but your execution plan may need to vary based on additional criteria.
          - [Script an execution plan approval](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ScptblAsmtExctnPlns.md) -- Each catalog item has an associated execution plan, used whenever an item of that type is ordered; if no plan is specified, the default plan is used. This default is effective for most organizations, but your execution plan may need to vary based on additional criteria.
        - [Using regular expressions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_RegularExpressionsInScripts.md) -- JavaScript regular expressions automatically use an enhanced regex engine, which provides improved performance and supports all behaviors of standard regular expressions as defined by Mozilla JavaScript. The enhanced regex engine supports using Java syntax in regular expressions.
          - [Using Java syntax in Javascript regex](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/r_JvSyntxJaScrRegex.md) -- The enhanced regex engine includes an additional flag to allow Java syntax to be used in JavaScript regular expressions.
          - [Convert SNC regex to enhanced regex](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/t_ConvertSNCRegexExpressions.md) -- When you upgrade to Eureka Patch 5 or later releases, you should convert scripts that use the SNC.Regex API to use regular JavaScript expressions.
        - [Scriptable service catalog variables](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ScriptableServiceCatalogVariables.md) -- You can use scripting to reference any request item variable from a table in scoped and non-scoped environment.
        - [Setting a GlideRecord variable to 'NULL'](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/r_SettingAGlideRecordVariableToNull.md) -- GlideRecord variables (including current) are initially null in the database. Setting these back to an empty string, a space, or the JavaScript null value will not result in a return to this initial state.
        - [Schedule Pages](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_SchedulePages.md) -- A schedule page is a record that contains a collection of scripts that allow for custom generation of a calendar or timeline display.
          - [Form](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_SchedulePages.md) -- A schedule page is a record that contains a collection of scripts that allow for custom generation of a calendar or timeline display.
          - [Timeline schedule pages](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_SchedulePages.md) -- A schedule page is a record that contains a collection of scripts that allow for custom generation of a calendar or timeline display.
          - [Timeline schedule page example](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_SchedulePages.md) -- A schedule page is a record that contains a collection of scripts that allow for custom generation of a calendar or timeline display.
        - [XMLDocument script object](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_XMLDocumentScriptObject.md) -- A JavaScript object wrapper for parsing and extracting XML data from an XML document (String).
          - [XMLHelper](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_XMLHelper.md) -- The XML helper script include makes it easy to parse XML in scripts.
        - [JavaScript engine](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_JS_engine_upgrade.md) -- The JavaScript engine that evaluates server-side scripts supports the ECMAScript 2021 (ES12) standard.
          - [Modes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_JS_modes.md) -- JavaScript mode is a design and runtime setting for custom applications and scripts. To support existing server-side scripts and new scripts developed to the ECMAScript 2021 standard, the JavaScript engine has three modes: ECMAScript 2021 (ES12), ES5 Standards, and Compatibility.
            - [Considerations for switching JavaScript modes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/considerations-switching-javascript-mode.md) -- Switching the JavaScript mode for an application or script might change the behavior of existing scripts. Review some examples of behavior changes before switching JavaScript modes or to troubleshoot any issues that you experience after switching.
            - [Set the JavaScript mode for an application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/set-javascript-mode-application.md) -- Configure which ECMAScript features can be used in an application by selecting the JavaScript mode.
            - [Turn on ECMAScript 2021 (ES12) mode for a script](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/set-es12-mode-scripts.md) -- Use the latest JavaScript features supported with ECMAScript 2021 (ES12) mode in server-side scripts in applications that use ES5 Standards mode or Compatibility mode.
          - [Supported features](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/javascript-engine-feature-support.md) -- Compare ECMAScript features between the ECMAScript 2021 (ES12) and ES5 Standards JavaScript modes in Zurich. Both modes support a subset of ECMAScript features.

        - [Client scripts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/client-scripts.md) -- Client scripts allow the system to run JavaScript on the client (web browser) when client-based events occur, such as when a form loads, after form submission, or when a field changes value.
        - [UI scripts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_UIScripts.md) -- UI scripts provide a way to package client-side JavaScript into a reusable form, similar to how script includes store server-side JavaScript. Administrators can create UI scripts and run them from client scripts and other client-side script objects and from HTML code.
          - [Global scripts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_UIScripts.md) -- UI scripts provide a way to package client-side JavaScript into a reusable form, similar to how script includes store server-side JavaScript.             - [<g:ui_checkbox/>](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ExtensionsToJellySyntax.md) -- Apache's Jelly syntax is used to render forms, lists, UI pages, and many other things rendered in ServiceNow.
            - [<g:dialog_buttons_ok_cancel/>](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ExtensionsToJellySyntax.md) -- Apache's Jelly syntax is used to render forms, lists, UI pages, and many other things rendered in ServiceNow.
            - [<g:ui_reference/>](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ExtensionsToJellySyntax.md) -- Apache's Jelly syntax is used to render forms, lists, UI pages, and many other things rendered in ServiceNow.
            - [Ampersand](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ExtensionsToJellySyntax.md) -- Apache's Jelly syntax is used to render forms, lists, UI pages, and many other things rendered in ServiceNow.
            - [And](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ExtensionsToJellySyntax.md) -- Apache's Jelly syntax is used to render forms, lists, UI pages, and many other things rendered in ServiceNow.
            - [Less than](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ExtensionsToJellySyntax.md) -- Apache's Jelly syntax is used to render forms, lists, UI pages, and many other things rendered in ServiceNow.
            - [Whitespace](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ExtensionsToJellySyntax.md) -- Apache's Jelly syntax is used to render forms, lists, UI pages, and many other things rendered in ServiceNow.
            - [Spaces](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ExtensionsToJellySyntax.md) -- Apache's Jelly syntax is used to render forms, lists, UI pages, and many other things rendered in ServiceNow.
            - [Tracing Jelly](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_ExtensionsToJellySyntax.md) -- Apache's Jelly syntax is used to render forms, lists, UI pages, and many other things rendered in ServiceNow.
      - [Debugging scripts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/script-debug-overview.md) -- Debug scripts using session logs and ServiceNow AI Platform debugging tools such as a walk-through script debugger and error messages that display in the UI.
        - [Script Debugger](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/script-debugger.md) -- The Script Debugger enables users with the script_debugger role to debug server-side JavaScript. Users with the log_debugger role can use the Session Log to view and download required logs.
          - [Access the Script Debugger](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/launch-script-debugger.md) -- Developers can access the Script Debugger from the application navigator, Studio, or from the syntax editor.
          - [Script Debugger user interface](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/parts-script-debugger-interface.md) -- The Script Debugger user interface (UI) displays information about breakpoints set, the call stack and line number of the currently executing script line, details about variables and transactions, and status of console.
            - [Script Tracer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/script-tracer.md) -- The Script Tracer can help you filter your debugging search to quickly narrow down script problems. You can identify lines of scripts in the Glide record that have undergone change during execution. Finding those specific lines of scripts rather than doing a wide search helps save time and improves productivity.
            - [Script Debugger status](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/script-debugger-status.md) -- The Script Debugger status determines what debugging actions are available and what information it can display.
            - [Transaction details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/transaction-details.md) -- The Script Debugger displays transaction details for the current paused user session.
              - [Available transaction details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/transaction-details.md) -- The Script Debugger displays transaction details for the current paused user session.
            - [Script Debugger controls](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/step-through-controls.md) -- After the Script Debugger pauses a script, use the step-through controls to move between script lines and move between scripts in the call stack. Use the Console controls to expand console, collapse console, clear console, and rerun expressions.
          - [Evaluate expressions at runtime](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/evaluate-expressions.md) -- Define, declare, and verify new variables and functions while you debug a script in runtime using Console. The script execution must be paused to use Console.
          - [Use breakpoints](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/set-remove-breakpoints.md) -- Set breakpoints or conditional breakpoints to pause scripts at specific lines, and remove breakpoints when you are done debugging them.
          - [Use logpoints](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/set-remove-logpts.md) -- Set breakpoints or conditional logpoints to log messages to the console at specific lines, and remove logpoints when you are done debugging them.
          - [Multiple developer support](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/multiple-developer-support.md) -- The Script Debugger allows multiple developers to debug their own transactions without affecting each other.
          - [Impersonation support](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/impersonation-support.md) -- You can use the Script Debugger while impersonating another user, but only if the impersonated user has the script_debugger role and has read access to the target script.
          - [Scripts - Background support](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/scripts-background-support.md) -- The Scripts - Background module does not support setting breakpoints directly in the script field. You can however, set breakpoints in the script objects called or triggered by the Scripts - Background module.
          - [Domain separation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/domain-separation-script-debugging.md) -- Domain separation is supported in Script Debugger. Domain separation enables you to separate data, processes, and administrative tasks into logical groupings called domains. You can control several aspects of this separation, including which users can see and access data.
        - [Debugging sessions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_SessionDebug.md) -- Enable session debugging to display debugging messages in the user interface.
          - [Display logs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_SessionDebug.md) -- Enable session debugging to display debugging messages in the user interface.
        - [Debugging applications](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_DebuggingApplications.md) -- Application developers can display debug messages about configuration records to help them troubleshoot issues. The Debug Scopes module provides information about the system switching between custom applications to run server-side scripts.
          - [Debugging scopes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_DebuggingApplications.md) -- Application developers can display debug messages about configuration records to help them troubleshoot issues. The Debug Scopes module provides information about the system switching between custom applications to run server-side scripts.
        - [Debugging business rules](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/r_DebuggingBusinessRules.md) -- Debugging business rules can be achieved with resources available in the ServiceNow product.
        - [Debugging classifications](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/r_DebuggingClassifications.md) -- You must add a system property to enable classification debugging.
        - [JS Code Coverage Debug](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/js-code-coverage-debug.md) -- JS Code Coverage Debug allows administrators and application developers to log the server-side scripts triggered during a user session and then review which lines of code the system ran.
          - [Highlighting](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/js-code-coverage-debug.md) -- JS Code Coverage Debug allows administrators and application developers to log the server-side scripts triggered during a user session and then review which lines of code the system ran.
          - [Activate](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/js-code-coverage-debug.md) -- JS Code Coverage Debug allows administrators and application developers to log the server-side scripts triggered during a user session and then review which lines of code the system ran.
          - [Use](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/js-code-coverage-debug.md) -- JS Code Coverage Debug allows administrators and application developers to log the server-side scripts triggered during a user session and then review which lines of code the system ran.
        - [JavaScript debug window](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_JavaScriptDebugWindow.md) -- The JavaScript debug window appears in a bottom pane of the user interface when an administrator turns on debugging.
          - [Access the JavaScript debug window](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/t_UsingTheJavaScriptDebugWindow.md) -- The JavaScript debug window enables access to the JavaScript Log and the Field Watcher tools.
          - [Watching fields](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_FieldWatcher.md) -- The field watcher tool tracks and displays all actions that the system performs on a selected form field.
            - [Use field watcher](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_FieldWatcher.md) -- The field watcher tool tracks and displays all actions that the system performs on a selected form field.
              - [Tab details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_FieldWatcher.md) -- The field watcher tool tracks and displays all actions that the system performs on a selected form field.
              - [Watch hidden fields](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_FieldWatcher.md) -- The field watcher tool tracks and displays all actions that the system performs on a selected form field.
            - [Viewing watched field info](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_FieldWatcher.md) -- The field watcher tool tracks and displays all actions that the system performs on a selected form field.
          - [Writing to the debug log](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_WritingToTheDebugLog.md) -- To write to the debug log in your client-side JavaScript, or UI policies, make a call to the global function jslog().
            - [Debug UI policies](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_WritingToTheDebugLog.md) -- To write to the debug log in your client-side JavaScript, or UI policies, make a call to the global function jslog().
            - [Access the JavaScript log](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_WritingToTheDebugLog.md) -- To write to the debug log in your client-side JavaScript, or UI policies, make a call to the global function jslog().
      - [Packages Call Removal Tool](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_PackagesCallRemovalTool.md) -- The Packages Call Removal Tool provides modules to identify fields that might contain scripts, find scripts that contain Packages calls to ServiceNow Java classes, and to examine proposed script changes that eliminate those Packages calls.
        - [Activate](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_PackagesCallRemovalTool.md) -- The Packages Call Removal Tool provides modules to identify fields that might contain scripts, find scripts that contain Packages calls to ServiceNow Java classes, and to examine proposed script changes that eliminate those Packages calls.
        - [Find a Packages call](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_PackagesCallRemovalTool.md) -- The Packages Call Removal Tool provides modules to identify fields that might contain scripts, find scripts that contain Packages calls to ServiceNow Java classes, and to examine proposed script changes that eliminate those Packages calls.
        - [Glide object name](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_PackagesCallRemovalTool.md) -- The Packages Call Removal Tool provides modules to identify fields that might contain scripts, find scripts that contain Packages calls to ServiceNow Java classes, and to examine proposed script changes that eliminate those Packages calls.
        - [Glide object replacement list](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_PackagesCallRemovalTool.md) -- The Packages Call Removal Tool provides modules to identify fields that might contain scripts, find scripts that contain Packages calls to ServiceNow Java classes, and to examine proposed script changes that eliminate those Packages calls.
        - [Error types](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/scripts/c_PackagesCallRemovalTool.md) -- The Packages Call Removal Tool provides modules to identify fields that might contain scripts, find scripts that contain Packages calls to ServiceNow Java classes, and to examine proposed script changes that eliminate those Packages calls.
    - [Web services](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_AvailableWebServices.md) -- HTTP-based web services allow diverse applications to talk to each other. ServiceNow supports both inbound (provider) and outbound (consumer) web services.
      - [Inbound](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/inbound-web-services.md) -- You can use any of several web services to integrate with ServiceNow applications, including REST, SOAP, JSONv2, and RSS.
        - [SOAP web service](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_SOAPWebService.md) -- Simple Object Access Protocol (SOAP) is an XML-based protocol for accessing web services over HTTP.
          - [Scripted Services](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_ScriptedWebServices.md) -- Scripted SOAP web services allow a ServiceNow administrator to create custom SOAP web services.
            - [Creating](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/createSOAPwebservice.md) -- Follow these examples to create a new scripted SOAP web service.
            - [Customize response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_CustomizeResponse.md) -- Follow this example to customize and control the XML payload of a SOAP response.
            - [Creating with static WSDLs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/createSOAPwebserviceStaticWSDL.md) -- Follow these examples to create a scripted SOAP web service using a static WSDL.
              - [Create a service](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_CreateStaticWSDLScriptWebService.md) -- To use a static WSDL, create a scripted web service.
                - [Example](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_StaticWSDLScriptedWebService.md) -- This example demonstrates the processing script for the FakeStockValue web service.
              - [Create a static WSDL](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_CreateAStaticWSDL.md) -- Create a static WSDL with the required format to override the standard WSDL for your scripted web service.
                - [Example](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_StaticWSDLExample.md) -- This example demonstrates the FakeStockValue WSDL.
              - [Create a static WSDL script include](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_CreateAStatickWSDLScriptInclude.md) -- Create a script include to define the majority of the code used to process static WSDL requests.
                - [Example](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_StaticWSDLScriptInclude.md) -- This example demonstrates the FakeStockValue script include that implements much of the static WSDL behavior.
              - [Using static WSDLs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_UsingAStaticWSDL.md) -- Load the static WSDL into a SOAP client to make requests to the SOAP web service.
          - [Direct services](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_DirectWebServices.md) -- A direct web service is available for any table in the system if the correct access control list is configured.
            - [Modifying query responses with forms](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/using-forms-limit-extend-query-resp.md) -- On occasion, there is a need to limit the number of field values that a SOAP query returns.
            - [Returning reference variable display values](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/return-display-reference-variables.md) -- When you query a record using a get or getRecords function, the instance returns all fields associated with that record. The fields are often reference fields that contain a sys_id for a record on another table.
            - [Clearing instance values](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/clearing-values-target.md) -- You can pass an empty value through a SOAP parameter to clear the respective value in the target instance.
            - [Retrieving journal entries](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/retrieving-journal-entries.md) -- To get the contents of a journal field, make a second soap request against the sys_journal_field table to pull the appropriate journal records back for the record in question.
            - [Retrieving choice fields](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/retrieving-choice-fields-direct-ws.md) -- To retrieve or set choice fields, use the choice Value, not the Label.
            - [Persisting an HTTP session](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/persist-session-all-soap-calls.md) -- In circumstances when a SOAP client makes many calls in a short amount of time, you may want to re-use a single HTTP session for all SOAP calls.
            - [API functions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_DirectWebServiceAPIFunctions.md) -- The standard SOAP API is a set of small, globally defined functions that can be performed on a targeted resource.
              - [Data Retrieval](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_DataRetrievalAPI.md) -- Data Retrieval API method summaries and descriptions.
                - [getKeys](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_GetKeys.md) -- Query the targeted table by example values and return a comma delimited sys_id list.
                - [getRecords](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_GetRecords.md) -- Query the targeted table by example values and return all matching records and their fields.
                - [get](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_Get.md) -- Query a single record from the targeted table by sys_id and return the record and its fields.
                - [aggregate](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_Aggregate.md) -- Query a table using an aggregate function including SUM, COUNT, MIN, MAX, LAST, and AVG.
              - [Data modification](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_DataModificationAPI.md) -- Data Modification API method summaries and descriptions.
                - [insert](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_Insert.md) -- Creates a new record for the table targeted in the URL.
                - [insertMultiple](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_InsertMultiple.md) -- Creates multiple new records for the table targeted in the URL.
                - [update](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_Update.md) -- Updates an existing record in the targeted table in the URL, identified by the mandatory sys_id field.
                - [deleteRecord](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_DeleteRecord.md) -- Delete a record from the targeted table by supplying its sys_id.
                - [deleteMultiple](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_DeleteMultiple.md) -- Delete multiple records from the targeted table by example values.
          - [Import sets](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/soap-web-service-import-sets.md) -- Web service import sets complement direct web services and scripted SOAP web services by providing a web service interface to import sets tables.
          - [AttachmentCreator](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_AttachmentCreatorSOAPWebService.md) -- Attach documents to records in ServiceNow by sending a SOAP message targeting the ecc_queue table.
          - [Overriding endpoints](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_OverridingTheSOAPEndpoint.md) -- The SOAP endpoint address where the SOAP message is posted is consistent with the endpoint of the WSDL.
          - [Enable HTTP compression](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_EnablingHTTPCompression.md) -- By default, the SOAP request is accepted un-compressed and the result of the request is returned un-compressed.
          - [Preventing empty message elements](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_PreventEmptyElementSOAPMsgs.md) -- By default, an instance does not omit empty elements, elements with NULL or NIL values, from SOAP messages.
          - [Insert related records](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_InsertRelatedRecordsUsingSOAP.md) -- Support is available for inserting hierarchical data into tables or web service import set tables. The hierarchical data in the Insert API is automatically mapped to related records of the targeted table.
            - [WSDL schema](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_WSDLSchemaWithRelatedRecords.md) -- When a WSDL for the target Incident table is requested with an additional parameter of hierarchical=true, the WSDL schema for the Insert function will reflect available related records that may participate in the hierarchical data payload.
            - [Hierarchical messages](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_HierarchicalSOAPMessage.md) -- When the SOAP message is constructed from the hierarchical web service described by the WSDL and invoked, it will create the incident, u_custom_comments, and u_comment_items records.
          - [Specify signed request requirements](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_SpecifyReqForSignedSOAPRequests.md) -- Use a SOAP security policy to specify whether the instance requires signed SOAP requests for all inbound SOAP traffic.
          - [Activate Enhanced Web Service Provider - Common](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_ActivateEnhancedWSPPlugin.md) -- Administrators can activate the Enhanced Web Service Provider - Common plugin to enable unsigned WS-Security requests and specify what authentication requirements SOAP requests have.
            - [Installed components](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_InstallWEnhancedWSPCommPlugin.md) -- The following components installed with the Enhanced Web Service Provider - Common plugin.
          - [Configure secutiry](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_ConfigureSOAPSecurity.md) -- Administrators can configure web service security for inbound SOAP requests made to the ServiceNow instance.
          - [Set default security policy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_SetTheSOAPDefaultSecurityPolicy.md) -- Set the SOAP default security policy.
          - [Create a security policy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_CreateANewSecurityPolicy.md) -- Administrators can specify which security profiles WS-Security communications must meet by creating a new security policy.
          - [Create a WS-Security profile](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_CreateANewWS-SecurityProfile.md) -- Create a new WS Security profile to define how to authenticate a web services message when WS-Security is enabled.
          - [Enforce strict security](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_EnforceStrictSecurityWebSvcConns.md) -- Strict security for web services requires that users meet Contextual Security requirements to access instance resources.
          - [Enable WS-Security verification](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_EnableWS-SecurityVerification.md) -- Administrators can enable Web Services Security (WSS) verification from the Web Services system properties.
            - [Make service accounts internal integration users](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_MarkSvcAcctsAsInternalIntegUsers.md) -- Allow internal integration communications to bypass the WSS authentication requirement by marking their user accounts as internal integration users.
          - [Debugging incoming envelopes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_DebuggingIncomingSOAPEnvelope.md) -- To capture incoming SOAP envelope XML in the system log, add the property glide.processor.debug.SOAPProcessor with a value of true.
          - [View a session log](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_ViewingASOAPSessionReport.md) -- You can view a user's log from a SOAP session.
          - [Authentication examples](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_BasicAuthenticationExamples.md) -- Samples of basic authentication code for several programming languages and versions.
          - [Example: WS-Security envelope header](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_SmplWSSecSOAPEnvHeader.md) -- An example of a valid WS-Security SOAP envelope header.
          - [WS-Security properties](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/ws-security-properties.md) -- These properties control the behavior of WS-Security X.509 tokens.
          - [WS-Security error messages](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_WS-SecurityErrorMessages.md) -- An instances produces one of the following error messages when it encounters an issue with a WS-security SOAP message.
          - [WS-Security reference](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/ws-security-reference.md) -- Support for WS-Security 1.1 in the form of WSS X.509 Token Profile and WSS Username Token Profile is available for incoming SOAP requests.
          - [Long-running request properties](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/LongRunningSOAPRequestProps.md) -- The following properties are available for long-running SOAP requests.
          - [Record retrieval limitations](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_RtvLrgNmbrRcrdSOAP.md) -- By default, a single SOAP request can retrieve a maximum of 250 records.
            - [Strategies](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_SOAPStrategies.md) -- Retrieve the information that you need and make your integration more efficient.
              - [Limiting results with filters](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_UseFiltersLimitNumberOfResults.md) -- One way to make your web service calls fit within the 250 record limit is to think about the design of your integrating application.
              - [Using local data storage](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_LclDtaStorePullDta.md) -- If a large amount of data needs to be queried often, and the data does not need to be real-time, perform a sync of the ServiceNow table that you're interested in with your integrating application's data store.
              - [Fetching and parsing data in code](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_FetchTheXMLData.md) -- If a local data store is not an option, another way to get the data is to call the CSV/XML processor directly and then parse the results.
        - [JSONv2 web service](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_JSONv2WebService.md) -- The ability to describe sets of data in JSON format is a natural extension to the JavaScript language.
          - [Object format](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_JSONObjectFormat.md) -- The JSON object is built in two structures.
          - [Response status](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_ResponseStatus.md) -- JSONv2 requests may return one of several response statuses.
          - [Setting the number of rows returned](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_SettingTheNumberOfRowsReturned.md) -- The following system property controls how many rows JSON returns with each query.
          - [Requiring incoming request authentication](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_ReqBasicAuthIncomJSONv2Requ.md) -- The following system property controls whether basic authentication is required for incoming JSONv2 requests.
          - [Action parameters](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_ActionParameters.md) -- Action parameters are separate and different from data parameters because they specify the action to take when the JSON object parameter is part of an HTTP GET or POST request.
          - [Data Retrieval API](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_DataRetrievalAPI.md) -- Query for data by issuing an HTTPS GET request to the instance.
          - [Data Modification API](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_DataModificationAPI.md) -- Modify data using the JSON web service by sending an HTTPS POST request to the instance.
        - [RSS web service](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/p_RSS.md) -- RSS (Rich Site Summary) is a format for delivering web-based information that changes regularly.
          - [Feed generator](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_RSSFeedGenerator.md) -- ServiceNow supports the dynamic generation of RSS feeds.
            - [Limiting results with a view](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_LimitingResultsWithAView.md) -- The description element in the returned RSS xml is constructed using the view as specified in the URL, when no view is specified, the default no-name view is used.
            - [Formatting results](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_FormattingResults.md) -- The description element in the returned RSS xml can be formatted by setting the URL parameter sysparm_format=true and specifying the format string in the property glide.rss.description_format.
            - [Basic authentication](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_BasicAuthentication.md) -- To enforce basic authentication on each request for an RSS feed, set the property glide.basicauth.required.rss to true.
            - [Title override](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_OverridingTheRSSTitle.md) -- You may optionally override the automatically generated title of the RSS feed by added the sysparm_title parameter to the request URL.
          - [Feed reader](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_RSSFeedReader.md) -- Create a scrolling RSS feed reader using a UI page.
            - [Scrollable areas](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_ScrollableAreas.md) -- A scrollable area is a div where contents scroll from the bottom up over time.
              - [Add scrolling form elements](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_AddingScrollingElementsInForms.md) -- You can add scrolling areas to forms as well as UI pages.
                - [Example](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_Priority1IncidentsExample.md) -- This example scrolling element demonstrates how to create a UI macro to a scrolling list of priority 1 incidents.
            - [Example](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_RSSFeedReaderExample.md) -- An example of how to set up an RSS feed reader using an RSS feed.
        - [Exporting records as complex data types](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/store-retrieve-records-complex-data-type.md) -- Use URL parameters to export table records and convert them into complex data types, such as JSON, XML, PDF, CSV, and XLS.
        - [Examples](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_InboundWebServiceExamples.md) -- Inbound web service examples demonstrate how to access ServiceNow web services.
          - [Java Apache Axis2](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_JAAWbSrvcsClntEx.md) -- Examples demonstrating an integration with Axis2 Version 1.4.
            - [Insert](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_JAAWbSrvcsClntExInst.md) -- An example class to insert an incident record.
            - [Update](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_JAAWbSrvcsClntExUpdt.md) -- An example of an Axis Client program that calls the getKeys function to query all incidents where the category is Hardware.
            - [Consume a ServiceNow Web Service](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_JAAWbSrvcsClntExAdv.md) -- Examples showing how to construct and use an Axis2 client to consume a ServiceNow Web Service.
          - [Microsoft .NET](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_MS.NETWebServicesClientExamples.md) -- Examples demonstrating an integration with Microsoft .NET Web Services Client.
          - [Perl](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_PerlWebServicesClientExamples.md) -- Examples demonstrating an integration with a Perl web services client.
          - [Python](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_PythonWebServicesClientExamples.md) -- Examples demonstrating an integration with a Python web services client.
          - [.NET tutorial](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_CSharpNETEndEnd.md) -- Examples demonstrating how to use .NET to consume a ServiceNow web service.
            - [Configure .NET](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_ConfigureCSharpWith.NET.md) -- Configure web services within ServiceNow.
            - [Call a web service](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_CallAWebServiceInVisualStudioNET.md) -- Call a web service using Visual Studio 2008.
              - [Use a service reference in an integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_UseAServiceReference.md) -- Use a wizard to add a service reference for a C Sharp integration.
              - [Use a web reference in an integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_UseAWebReference.md) -- Use a wizard to add a web reference for a C Sharp integration.
              - [Integration source code](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_SourceCode.md) -- After defining the source code, insert it.
            - [Results](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_Results.md) -- If you have followed the tutorial correctly, you should receive the result whether you used a Service Reference or a Web Reference.
            - [Troubleshoot a null response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_Troubleshoot.md) -- Receiving a null response from ServiceNow's web service.
          - [ASP.NET with redirect and cookies](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_ASPNETCSharpRdrctCks.md) -- This sample ASP.NET code creates a simple authentication portal and passes an unencrypted HTTP header as a cookie.
          - [ASP.NET unencrypted SSO](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_SampleASPScriptUnencryptedSSO.md) -- This sample ASP.NET code creates a simple authentication portal and passes an unencrypted HTTP header as a URL parameter.
      - [Outbound](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/outbound-web-services.md) -- You can use any of several outbound web services to integrate with ServiceNow applications, including REST and SOAP.
        - [REST](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_OutboundRESTWebService.md) -- ServiceNow outbound REST functionality allows you to retrieve, create, update, or delete data on a web services server that supports the REST architecture.
          - [Message elements](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/r_RESTMessageElements.md) -- An outbound REST message is composed of several elements, such as the endpoint and HTTP methods.
          - [Create a message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_ConfiguringARESTMessage.md) -- Send requests to a REST web service endpoint by creating a REST message record.
            - [Define an HTTP method](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_DefineAnHTTPMethod.md) -- Define an HTTP method such as GET or POST to send a request to a web service provider.
              - [Test a method](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_TestingMethods.md) -- Test an HTTP method for an outbound REST message to ensure that the request is valid and the response returns as expected.
            - [Define an HTTP header](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_DefineAnHTTPHeader.md) -- Define an HTTP header for a REST message or HTTP method to send that header with REST requests.
            - [Sending messages through a MID Server](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_OutboundRESTMIDServerSupport.md) -- You can configure a REST message HTTP method to be sent through a MID Server.
            - [Using special characters in URIs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_UsingSpecialCharactersInURIs.md) -- A REST function URI or function variable may use special characters, such as pipe (|) characters.
          - [Outbound REST authentication](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_OutboundRESTAuth.md) -- Outbound REST messages support multiple types of authentication.
            - [Configure a message with basic auth](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_ConfigureRESTMsgBasicAuth.md) -- Configure an outbound REST message to provide basic authentication credentials with each request.
              - [Create a basic auth profile](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_CreateABasicAuthProfile.md) -- Create a basic auth profile to specify basic authentication credentials for one or more REST messages.
            - [Configure a message with OAuth](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_ConfigureARESTMessageWithOAuth.md) -- You can configure an outbound REST message to send OAuth credentials with the request.
              - [Use a third-party OAuth provider](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_UseAThirdPartyOAuthProvider.md) -- Each client application must register with the instance to participate in OAuth authorization.
                - [Profiles and scopes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_OAuthProviderAndScope.md) -- In the OAuth provider scenario, profiles and scopes specify the grant type, authorization type, and level of access.
                  - [Specify a profile](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_SpecifyAnOAuthProfile.md) -- An OAuth profile includes the grant type that the third-party OAuth provider needs to obtain access to the restricted resource.
                  - [Specify a scope](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_SpecifyAnOAuthScope.md) -- Specify the OAuth scopes that you get from the provider. Scopes can be any level of access specified by the provider, such as read, write, or any string, including a URL.
              - [OAuth 2.0 profile tutorial: integrating with Google Contacts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_OAuth2ProfileTutorialGoogle.md) -- This tutorial explains how to use an OAuth 2.0 profile to authenticate an outbound REST message with Google to retrieve contact information.
                - [Configure the Google service as OAuth provider](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_OAuthDemoConfigureGoogle.md) -- Use the Google Developer Console to set up an OAuth 2.0 provider.
                - [Create a provider and profile](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_OAuthDemoCreateProvider.md) -- Set up the Google service as an OAuth provider in ServiceNow by entering your client information, Google API URLs, and configuring the OAuth profile.
                - [Create a message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/t_OAuthDemoCreateRESTMessage.md) -- Create a REST message and associated HTTP method to contact the Google service using the OAuth 2.0 profile.
            - [Mutual authentication](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/web-services/c_OutboundRESTMutualAuthentication.md) -- Mutual authentication causes the web service provider and consumer to authenticate with each other before communicating.
