Instructions are also provided for setting authentication when using cXML as the payload.
    - [Event Notification Management Open API Developer Guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/event-mgmt-notif_dev-guide.md) -- Use the Event Notification Management Open API to create, update, and delete event records in the Events [em_event] table.
      - [Extend the Event Notification Management Open API](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/event-mgmt-notif_dev-guide.md) -- Use the Event Notification Management Open API to create, update, and delete event records in the Events [em_event] table.
    - [GlideGeoPoint Developer Guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/geopoint-dev-guide.md) -- The GlideGeoPoint developer guide contains information about how to compute distances between latitude and longitude values (geopoints), return locations within given geopoint and radius values, and use additional functionality provided by supported GlideGeoPoint API functions.
      - [Create geopoints using the GlideGeoPoint API](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/geopoint-dev-guide.md) -- The GlideGeoPoint developer guide contains information about how to compute distances between latitude and longitude values (geopoints), return locations within given geopoint and radius values, and use additional functionality provided by supported GlideGeoPoint API functions.
      - [Read geopoint values using the GlideGeoPoint API](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/geopoint-dev-guide.md) -- The GlideGeoPoint developer guide contains information about how to compute distances between latitude and longitude values (geopoints), return locations within given geopoint and radius values, and use additional functionality provided by supported GlideGeoPoint API functions.
      - [Calculate the distance between two geopoints](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/geopoint-dev-guide.md) -- The GlideGeoPoint developer guide contains information about how to compute distances between latitude and longitude values (geopoints), return locations within given geopoint and radius values, and use additional functionality provided by supported GlideGeoPoint API functions.
      - [Filter the distance between two geopoints](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/geopoint-dev-guide.md) -- The GlideGeoPoint developer guide contains information about how to compute distances between latitude and longitude values (geopoints), return locations within given geopoint and radius values, and use additional functionality provided by supported GlideGeoPoint API functions.
    - [Mobile SDK Developer Guide - Android](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-overview.md) -- The ServiceNow Mobile SDK enables you to leverage the functionality and resources of the ServiceNow platform within your standalone mobile Android applications.
      - [NowSDK architecture](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-nowsdk-arch.md) -- The NowSDK is the top-level entity of the Mobile SDK class framework.
      - [Get started with Mobile SDK - Android](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-get-started.md) -- You must complete the tasks outlined in this section to prepare your development environment to support writing Android applications that integrate with the ServiceNow Mobile SDK.
        - [Download and install the Mobile SDK library](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-down-install-sdk.md) -- Before you can create mobile applications that interact with your ServiceNow instance, you must first download and install the ServiceNow Mobile SDK Android library.
        - [Install and configure the Mobile SDK scoped application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-install_config-sdk_plugin.md) -- Before writing any application that leverages the Mobile SDK, you must install and configure the Mobile SDK scoped application.
        - [Configure your project](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-config-project.md) -- Before writing any application that leverages the Mobile SDK for Android, you must configure your project to use the SDK.
        - [Configure certificate and create an OAuth Entity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-config-certificate.md) -- You must configure your ServiceNow instance to accept and exchange a JSON Web Token (JWT) for an OAuth access token. To enable this functionality you must install a certificate that matches the JWT provider’s private key on the ServiceNow instance that you will use as the JWT Token consumer and create an OAuth Entity on that instance.
        - [Configure guest user access](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-config-guest-access.md) -- To enable SDK services to be accessible by unauthenticated users, you must configure the Mobile SDK on your ServiceNow instance to allow guest user access. A guest user can view public web pages hosted in an instance using NowWeb, have a conversation as a guest using NowChat, access public data on the instance using NowTableService and NowGraphQLService, and access custom API services configured for unauthenticated users using NowAPIService.
      - [Initialize the NowSDK in your application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-init-nowsdk-in-app.md) -- In order to use the Mobile SDK in your application, you need to perform SDK initialization, authorization, and logging.
      - [Implement Virtual and Live Agent chat](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-imp-chat.md) -- The Mobile SDK enables you to easily implement Virtual and Live Agent chat services within your Android application.
        - [Pass NowChatConfiguration to modify the behavior of NowChat](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-chat_options.md) -- You can pass an optional NowChatConfiguration parameter when starting a chat session to modify some of the behavior of NowChat.
      - [Interact with table data in a ServiceNow instance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-interact_data_instance.md) -- The Mobile SDK enables you to interact with data in tables that reside on your ServiceNow instance. You can interact with this data through the ServiceNow REST Table API using the NowTableService() API that directly calls the REST interface, or through the NowGraphQLService() API that executes a specified GraphQL query against the REST GraphQL API.
        - [Configure return results data](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-config_results_returned.md) -- Within the Mobile SDK, when interacting with data from a ServiceNow instance through a REST endpoint, you can configure what data is passed back in the return results.
        - [Configure field write options](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-write_options.md) -- Within the Mobile SDK, when writing data to a ServiceNow instance through a REST endpoint, you can define field write options.
        - [Paginate return results record sets](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-paginate_return_records.md) -- Within the Mobile SDK, when interacting with data from a ServiceNow instance through a REST endpoint using the NowTableService, you can create a Paginator object that enables you to iterate over the records in the return results.
        - [Configure tables to work with guests](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/modsdk-and-config-guests.md) -- If you want guest users to be able to interact with data within a table on your ServiceNow instance, you must configure the table to be guest accessible.
      - [Interact with the public REST API on a ServiceNow instance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-interact-rest_api.md) -- The Mobile SDK provides functionality that enables Android applications to call the public REST API on a ServiceNow instance.
      - [Retrieve attachments and attachment metadata](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-retrieve_attach_metadata.md) -- The NowAttachmentService API enables you to perform CRUD operations on attachments and retrieve attachment metadata from your ServiceNow instance.
      - [Display web pages hosted on your ServiceNow instance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-display-web-pages.md) -- You can display web pages hosted within your ServiceNow instance in your native Android application.
      - [Configure push notifications](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-push-notification.md) -- Push notifications enable the sending of unsolicited messages (notifications) from a ServiceNow instance to a specific registered user and an application that leverages the ServiceNow Mobile SDK framework.
        - [Setting up push notifications on your ServiceNow instance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-setup-push-instance.md) -- For each Mobile SDK application that incorporates the Virtual Agent service and leverages push notifications, you must complete the following configuration process on your ServiceNow instance.
          - [Retrieve the Firebase push server key](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-retrieve-firebase-key.md) -- You must retrieve your unique Google Firebase push server key from your Firebase account so that you can associate it to your Mobile SDK applications that leverage push notifications.
          - [Add a push application record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-add-push-app.md) -- You must enter your Google Firebase Cloud Messaging server key in your push application record to use push notifications in a Mobile SDK application that leverages Virtual Agent and push notifications.
          - [Add a default push registration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-add-default-push-reg.md) -- Register the push notification so that all users of your application receive the push notifications by default.
          - [Add a push notification message content record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-add-push-msg-content.md) -- The push notification message content record contains the location users are directed to when they tap on a notification, as well as the appearance and layout of the notification.
          - [Add a push notification message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-add-push-notif-msg.md) -- Configure the message displayed to users when they receive their push notification.
          - [Modify the push notification](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-mod-push-not.md) -- Modify the Virtual Agent Message Push Notification record to include your application. This record is used to trigger notifications to the Virtual Agent. All applications that will push notifications to the Virtual Agent must be configured in this record.
        - [Setting up push notifications in your Android application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-setup-push-app.md) -- In addition to the tasks that you must perform on your ServiceNow instance to configure a push notification, you must also include specific code in your Android application.
      - [Use NowUIColoring to theme NowWebTheme and NowChatTheme](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-and-color-theming.md) -- The NowUIColoring interface contains all the colors used by all NowSDK modules.
    - [Mobile SDK Developer Guide - iOS](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-overview.md) -- The ServiceNow Mobile SDK enables you to leverage the functionality and resources of the ServiceNow platform within your standalone mobile iOS applications.
      - [NowSDK architecture](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-nowsdk-arch.md) -- The NowSDK is the top-level entity of the Mobile SDK class framework.
      - [Get started with Mobile SDK - iOS](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-get-started.md) -- You must complete the tasks outlined in this section to prepare your development environment to support writing iOS applications that integrate with the ServiceNow Mobile SDK.
        - [Download and install the Mobile SDK libraries](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-down-install-sdk-libs.md) -- Before you are able to create mobile applications that interact with your ServiceNow instance, you must first download and install the ServiceNow Mobile SDK libraries.
        - [Install and configure the Mobile SDK scoped application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-install_config-sdk_plugin.md) -- Before writing any application that leverages the Mobile SDK, you must install and configure the Mobile SDK scoped application.
        - [Configure your project](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-config-project.md) -- Before writing any application that leverages the Mobile SDK for iOS, you must configure your project to use the SDK.
        - [Configure certificate and create an OAuth Entity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-config-certificate.md) -- You must configure your ServiceNow instance to accept and exchange a JSON Web Token (JWT) for an OAuth access token. To enable this functionality you must install a certificate that matches the JWT provider’s private key on the ServiceNow instance that you will use as the JWT Token consumer and create an OAuth Entity on that instance.
        - [Configure guest user access](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-config-guest-access.md) -- To enable SDK services to be accessible by unauthenticated users, you must configure the Mobile SDK on your ServiceNow instance to allow guest user access. A guest user can view public web pages hosted in an instance using NowWeb, have a conversation as a guest using NowChat, access public data on the instance using NowTableService and NowGraphQLService, and access custom API services configured for unauthenticated users using NowAPIService.
      - [Initialize the NowSDK in your application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-init-nowsdk-in-app.md) -- To access the functionality provided by the NowSDK, you must first initialize the NowSDK in your application.
      - [Implement Virtual and Live Agent chat](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-imp-chat.md) -- The Mobile SDK enables you to easily implement Virtual and Live Agent chat services within your iOS application.
        - [Passing context variables to Live Agent and Virtual Agent chat](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-pass-con-variables.md) -- You can pass chat context variables when starting a chat session by passing the contextData parameter.
      - [Interact with table data in a ServiceNow instance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-interact_data_instance.md) -- The Mobile SDK enables you to interact with data in tables that reside on your ServiceNow instance. You can interact with this data through the ServiceNow REST Table API using the NowTableService() API that directly calls the REST interface, or through the NowGraphQLService() API that executes a specified GraphQL query against the REST GraphQL API.
        - [Configure return results data](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-config_results_returned.md) -- Within the Mobile SDK, when interacting with data from a ServiceNow instance through a REST endpoint, you can configure what data is passed back in the return results.
        - [Configure field write options](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-write_options.md) -- Within the Mobile SDK, when writing data to a ServiceNow instance through a REST endpoint, you can define field write options.
        - [Paginate return results record sets](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-paginate_return_records.md) -- Within the Mobile SDK, when interacting with data from a ServiceNow instance through a REST endpoint using the NowTableService, you can create a Paginator object that enables you to iterate over the records in the return results.
        - [Transform data](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-transform_data.md) -- The NowData framework provides the ability to easily transform data into records.
      - [Interact with the public REST API on a ServiceNow instance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-interact-rest_api.md) -- The Mobile SDK provides functionality that enables iOS applications to call the public REST API on a ServiceNow instance.
      - [Retrieve attachments and attachment metadata](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-retrieve_attach_metadata.md) -- The NowAttachmentService API enables you to perform CRUD operations on attachments and retrieve attachment metadata from your ServiceNow instance.
      - [Display web pages hosted on your ServiceNow instance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-display-web-pages.md) -- You can display web pages hosted within your ServiceNow instance in your native iOS application.
      - [Configure push notifications](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-push-notification.md) -- Push notifications enable the sending of unsolicited messages (notifications) from a ServiceNow instance to a specific registered user and an application that leverages the ServiceNow Mobile SDK framework.
        - [Setting up push notifications on your ServiceNow instance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-setup-push-instance.md) -- For each Mobile SDK application that incorporates the Virtual Agent service and leverages push notifications, you must complete the following configuration process on your ServiceNow instance.
          - [Generate and retrieve an iOS push certificate](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-retrieve-apple-cert.md) -- You must retrieve your unique iOS push certificate from your Apple Developer account so that you can associate it to your Mobile SDK applications that leverage push notifications.
          - [Add a push certificate record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-add_push_cert.md) -- After generating your iOS push certificate, you must save the push certificate record to your instance. The push certificate is stored in the X.509 Certificate [sys_certificate] table.
          - [Add a push application record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-add-push-app.md) -- You must create a push application record and enter your Apple Push Notification certificate in that record to use push notifications in a Mobile SDK application that leverages Virtual Agent and push notifications.
          - [Add a default push registration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-add-default-push-reg.md) -- Register the push notification so that all users of your application receive the push notifications by default.
          - [Add a push notification message content record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-add-push-msg-content.md) -- The push notification message content record contains the location users are directed to when they tap on a notification, as well as the appearance and layout of the notification.
          - [Add a push notification message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-add-push-notif-msg.md) -- Configure the message displayed to users when they receive their push notification.
          - [Modify the push notification](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-mod-push-not.md) -- Modify the Virtual Agent Message Push Notification record to include your application. This record is used to trigger notifications to the Virtual Agent. All applications that will push notifications to the Virtual Agent must be configured in this record.
        - [Setting up push notifications in your iOS application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-setup-push-app.md) -- In addition to the tasks that you must perform on your ServiceNow instance to configure a push notification, you must also include specific code in your iOS application.
      - [Work with coders and Codable models](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-work-coder-codable_mod.md) -- The iOS implementation of the Mobile SDK provides the additional functionality of coders and Codable models.
      - [Archive your application for publishing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-archiving-app.md) -- It is important to disable rebuild from bitcode when archiving your application for publication for release.
      - [Use NowUIColoring when theming NowWebThemeable and NowChatThemeable](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/mobsdk-ios-use_nowUIcoloring.md) -- The NowUIColoring interface contains all the colors used by all NowSDK modules.
    - [Producer Event Notification Framework developer guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/prod_evt_not-dev_guide-overview.md) -- The Producer Event Notification Framework for Telecommunications, Media, and Technology enables the external distribution of events, such as trouble tickets, using a message-bus implemented in your network.
      - [Create outbound Order Management notifications using the open message bus](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/eventMgmtNotif-use-open-mssg-bus.md) -- Learn how to produce an outbound notification from a ServiceNow instance using the open message bus. Customers can consume the details of the notification from the message bus in their external system.
      - [Add a business rule for a new Order Management event](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/orderMgmt-add-business-rule.md) -- Learn how to configure business rules on a ServiceNow instance to recognize new Order Management events and push them into the notification system.
      - [Configure the Producer Event Notification Framework to use the Hermes Messaging Service](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/prod_evt_not-dev_gd-cfg_herm_bus.md) -- If you are using the Hermes Messaging Service in your instance, you must configure the Producer Event Notification Framework for the Kafka message-bus.
      - [Configure the Producer Event Notification Framework to use the Open Message Bus](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/prod_evt_not-dev_gd-cfg_open_bus.md) -- If you have deployed or plan to deploy the Open Message Bus in your instance, you must configure the Producer Event Notification Framework for this message-bus. You can deploy this message-bus for both cloud and on-premise instances.
      - [Configure trouble ticket notifications using the Producer Event Notification Framework](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/prd_evt_not-dev_gd-cfg_trbl_tkt_not.md) -- The base implementation of trouble ticket notification includes a set of trouble ticket events to demonstrate the capabilities of the system.
        - [Add a business rule for a new trouble ticket event](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/prd_evt_not-dev_gd-add_bus_rule.md) -- When adding a new trouble ticket event to your implementation, you must add a business rule to your ServiceNow instance.
        - [Generate the payload for a new trouble ticket event](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/prd_evt_not-dev_gd-gen_evt_pay.md) -- When adding a trouble ticket event to your implementation, you must create a function that generates the required TMF-compliant payload for that event type. In addition, you must add that function to the case statement that directs the logic to this function when processing this type of event record.
        - [Modify a trouble ticket event payload](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/prd_evt_not-dev_gd-mod-evt-pay.md) -- The base trouble ticket event notification implementation provides multiple default trouble ticket event type examples. You may need to alter the payloads for these events within your implementation to meet your actual needs. You may also need to configure the payload for any new trouble ticket events that you add to your implementation.
      - [Override Event Management Topic Open API functionality](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/prod_evt_not-dev_gd-over_evt_top.md) -- The Event Management Topic Open API enables you to save topic records in the Topic [sn_api_notif_mgmt_topic] table after validating the topic payload.
    - [Product Order Open API Developer Guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/product-order_dev-guide.md) -- Use the Product Order Open API to create, update, and retrieve product order information.
      - [Extending the Product Order Open API](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/product-order_dev-guide.md) -- Use the Product Order Open API to create, update, and retrieve product order information.
    - [Resource Inventory Open API Developer Guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/resource-inventory-dev-guide.md) -- Use the Resource Inventory Open API to create, retrieve, update, and delete resources such as interface cards, logical and physical connections, network sites, and more.
      - [Extending the Resource Inventory Open API](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/resource-inventory-dev-guide.md) -- Use the Resource Inventory Open API to create, retrieve, update, and delete resources such as interface cards, logical and physical connections, network sites, and more.
    - [ScopedCacheManager API Developer Guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/scoped-caches.md) -- Scoped caches allow customers to store custom data in memory for faster access, and define when it becomes invalidated. This can be useful when working with data takes longer to obtainthat from scratch, such as the results of a query across multiple tables. Use the ScopedCacheManager API to retrieve, set, and flush cache data for scoped applications.
      - [Creating a scoped cache and table pair](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/scoped-caches.md) -- Scoped caches allow customers to store custom data in memory for faster access, and define when it becomes invalidated. This can be useful when working with data takes longer to obtainthat from scratch, such as the results of a query across multiple tables. Use the ScopedCacheManager API to retrieve, set, and flush cache data for scoped applications.
    - [Service Order Open API Developer Guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/service-order_dev-guide.md) -- Use the Service Order Open API to create, update, and retrieve service order information.
      - [Extending the Service Order Open API](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/zurich/markdown/api-reference/developer-guides/service-order_dev-guide.md) -- Use the Service Order Open API to create, update, and retrieve service order information.
